capture_survey_comment: thin and a bit sluggish when handled. gray pallor. swam fine on release.
badly decomposed, likely died over winter. egg jellies protruding from what remains of abdomen.
frog was quite skinny. lacked righting reflex when flipped on back. seemed very sluggish... slow and weak response when handled
seems to be well fed, but lots of loose skin on belly.
likely has been dead for at least 1 month.
very decomposed, likely dead many months, some egg mass visible, recovered pit tag. same as seen on east shore last night but did not scan them.
skinny frog. when sitting on shore, was holding its rear legs out from the body. chytridiomycosis
unrecognizably deformed and bloated. clumped together with dead eggs
changed recorded sex to male. weakly developed thumb pads, but large forearms.
has been dead for at least 1 week, based on degree of decomposition.
skin has a bit of a grayish hue, noticeably atypical. indication of chytridiomycosis or something else
